title: Boulder Co Vlog 4/5/18: Rode to Loveland 75.88mi 4:33:52  3,187ft   161SS

enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs after completing a 75.88-mile bike ride from Boulder to Loveland, CO — his first big ride in a while. He recaps the ride logistics, food choices, and physical sensations, then describes spending the afternoon at his mom's house helping with chores and taking his brother's dog to the park.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Keep riding until too weak to go effectively rather than taking prescribed rest days
Real food like a breakfast burrito is better than power bars or oatmeal for long rides
Will stick to cycling until his knee blows out, then switch to running and swimming
Being an athlete heightens everything — food, water, sleep all feel better when your body truly needs them

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I didn't sleep last night at all, but I laid there and made sure to rest.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I got up at 5 AM and was ready to go by sunrise at 6:45 AM.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: It was already light enough to ride a half hour before the sun officially arrived.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I could have been out the door at 6 AM today, even though it was a little cold.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I had all the clothes I needed to stay warm in 40-degree weather.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I ate a bunch of fruit for breakfast before riding my bike.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I rode my bike to Loveland for the first time, which was exciting.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I went downtown and got a breakfast burrito and hash browns.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I rode up to the canyon and back to Boulder, enjoying the scenery and other riders.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Today was my first big ride since I started riding again.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I was super fired up about riding this morning.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I rode 75 miles today in four and a half hours, averaging about 16 miles per hour.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My watt pedals didn't register properly, likely because the battery is dying.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I had a suffer score of 161, which is considered extreme.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The suffer score suggests I should take three to four days to recover.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The best way to recover is to keep riding until you're too weak to ride the next day.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My left knee was hurting and felt loose, while my right side was tight and fresh.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The pain in my left knee was a sweet pain, almost like getting kicked in the nuts.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My left shoulder and hip were also bothering me during the ride.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Getting a breakfast burrito was a good strategy because it's easier to eat than power bars or oatmeal.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I don't know how many power bars I need for long rides because my body works differently now.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: After the ride, I went to my mom's house and helped with her taxes and cleaning.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I accompanied my mom to the dog park with my brother's dog.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm feeling the effects of being an athlete again, like being really tired and needing sleep.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Everything feels heightened when riding a lot—food, water, and sleep are all more enjoyable.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I won't run or swim tomorrow because I'm focusing on being a triathlete.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I expect my knee to blow out sooner because I'm older, and I'll switch sports when that happens.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: When my knee blows out, it'll be a sharp shooting pain that makes it hard to extend fully.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm looking forward to trying other sports when my knee gives out.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I wish I had started this training two weeks earlier.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm excited to sleep tonight because I'm exhausted from the ride.
